# Inspection Address Helper Integration: Initial Grill Plan

## Source Inputs

- `docs/project_status/devnotes.md`, item 10
- `docs/project_status/requiredlifecycle.md`, inspection-address enrichment step
- `docs/planning/archive/project_status_retired_2026_06_08/features/inspection_location_readiness/`
- Existing documents under `docs/planning/inspection_address_helper/`

## Current Understanding

The helper should try location metadata first, then OCR, then possible vision/landmark identification and approved image corpus matching. Existing project rules say inspection address must be source-backed and not guessed; missing address handling follows provider policy rather than a silent fallback.

## Resolved Decisions

- Physical inspection address is preferred wherever one can reasonably be established.
- The full inspection address assistance flow should run before falling back to `Image Based Assessment`.
- Some providers should always default to `Image Based Assessment`; this requires a provider setting/option.
- For other providers, falling back to `Image Based Assessment` is acceptable when no physical inspection address can be established, but it is less desirable than finding a proper address.
- The fallback must be recorded as a deliberate location mode/reason, not as a silent guessed address.
- Provider inspection behavior should be controlled by `inspection_location_policy`.
- `inspection_location_policy = always_image_based` means always use Image Based Assessment for that provider.
- `inspection_location_policy = prefer_address` means run the assistance flow and autofill a physical address where possible, with visible markers showing how it was populated.
- `inspection_location_policy = required_address` means Image Based Assessment cannot be used except by Management override.
- For `prefer_address`, acceptable ranked address evidence includes source document text, provider/principal/garage corpus, OCR text from images, phone number or business signage lookup, staff-entered correction history, and EXIF/GPS metadata when present.
- EXIF/GPS metadata should be treated as useful but often unavailable because WhatsApp may strip it.
- Landmark/vision identification may support an address autofill candidate but should not be enough by itself to progress the case to EVA unless the Work Provider's automation mode and evidence gates permit it.
- Inspection-address auto-population should be governed by the Work Provider's Provider Automation Mode.
- Default auto-population should allow only high-confidence source document text or a provider rule with an exact known address.
- Corpus, OCR, phone/business-signage, and staff-history combinations may autofill fields, but the fields remain visibly marked for staff review unless the Work Provider is in `AI Auto` or `Full auto` and all gates pass.
- EXIF/GPS and vision/landmark evidence should never auto-populate alone.
- EVA progression is the important gate: auto-filled values may populate the case, but must show visible markers notifying staff what populated each value, such as PDF extraction, AI-filled, location data, corpus data, DVLA/DVSA, or staff entry.
- If the Work Provider is `No auto`, staff enter the data manually.
- If the Work Provider is `Review auto`, assistance tools may populate fields, but staff acknowledgement is required before EVA submission.
- If the Work Provider is `AI Auto`, assistance tools may populate fields and the case may proceed only after the strict AI review passes.
- If the Work Provider is `Full auto`, assistance tools may populate fields and the case may proceed straight to EVA when every provider, evidence, readiness, credential, and policy gate passes.
- For `required_address`, using Image Based Assessment still requires Management override.
- Missing EXIF/GPS on WhatsApp images is normal and should not be shown as a warning by itself.
- Missing WhatsApp metadata lowers confidence only because the EXIF/GPS evidence signal is absent.
- The assistant should then rely on OCR, registration match, provider/garage corpus, signage, phone numbers, and staff review.
- When the helper identifies a garage or business but does not yet have a full six-line EVA address, it may use vetted corpus information or governed web search to complete the address.
- Web-search-derived address completion must preserve evidence/source details and follow the Work Provider's Provider Automation Mode.
- Completed addresses must still normalize to the six-line EVA address format before readiness or submission.
- The helper should run automatically during intake/parser processing when an address is missing or incomplete.
- The helper should run again during the automation pipeline before EVA readiness.
- Staff should have a manual `Run address assistance` action on the case page.
- Live AI or web search must not run during ordinary page render.
- First-class guidance and feature docs should use the provider policy model: `always_image_based`, `prefer_address`, and `required_address`.

## Resolved Question Status

Initial inspection address helper questions are resolved. Documentation should continue to align to the provider policy model before implementation work proceeds.

## Recommended Starting Position

Run the helper as review-required enrichment after deterministic extraction and before EVA readiness evaluation. It may suggest addresses with evidence, but staff, Management override, or approved provider rules must confirm before readiness is unblocked.
